title: Your First Draft: Write, Revise, Publish a Flash Fiction Piece
description: Write a complete flash fiction story (500 words) from discovery draft through revision to finished piece. Touches concepts from all 5 wings.

steps.instruction: Read the drafting and discovery concept. Set a timer for 15 minutes. Write anything -- a scene, a moment, a fragment. Do not stop. Do not edit. Do not judge. When the timer ends, you have a zero draft.
steps.expectedOutcome: You have written at least 300 words of raw material without internal censorship
steps.hint: If you are stuck, write about being stuck. Start with 'I remember...' or 'She said...' or describe the room you are in right now

steps.instruction: Read your zero draft and underline the one sentence that feels most alive. That sentence is the seed of your story. Read the conflict types concept -- what conflict could grow from that sentence? Identify a central conflict and write a one-sentence story premise.
steps.expectedOutcome: You have identified a living seed in your draft and articulated a conflict that can drive a 500-word story
steps.hint: Conflict does not have to be dramatic -- 'a woman decides whether to open an old letter' is conflict enough for flash fiction

steps.instruction: Read the imagery and sensory detail concept. In your next draft, include at least one piece of vivid sensory detail per paragraph. Avoid the word 'feel.' Instead of 'she felt sad,' write what she sees, hears, or does that shows sadness.
steps.expectedOutcome: You have a second draft that shows rather than tells, with at least 3 specific sensory details
steps.hint: Read your draft aloud. Where does it feel vague or told rather than shown? Those are the passages to rewrite with specific images

steps.instruction: Read the peer feedback concept. Share your draft with one reader (a friend, classmate, or parent). Ask them to tell you: what was the moment that had the most impact? Where did they lose the thread? What do they want to know more about?
steps.expectedOutcome: You have specific reader feedback to guide your final revision
steps.hint: Do not defend your choices while they are talking. Just listen and take notes. You can disagree later in your revision choices

steps.instruction: Write your final version. Read the voice development concept. Does this story sound like you? Revise one paragraph to make it sound more distinctly yours -- a specific observation, a particular sentence rhythm, a detail only you would notice.
steps.expectedOutcome: You have a finished 500-word flash fiction piece that has gone through discovery, imagery revision, feedback, and voice refinement
